mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production signal expanding economic output and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as investors price in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5 percent, it indicates a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer prices and high federal funds rate compress real interest rates and tighten financial conditions, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a significantly negative coefficient in a regression on 60-day forward SPX returns at the 5% level over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic growth measured by GDP growth above 2% quarter-over-quarter, rising retail sales confirm strong consumer demand and boost corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and RSAFS is rising, the forward 20-day SPX return is negative more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly payroll gains above 200,000 indicate a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and economic momentum, driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The success rate of positive 5-day returns after PAYEMS increases above 200k is below 55% over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect healthy consumer spending, a key driver of US economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day forward SPX return following positive RSAFS monthly changes is not significantly greater than zero at the 10% level over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising unemployment rate and a federal funds rate above 4% creates a tightening cycle that depresses aggregate demand and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: rising unemployment with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: In episodes with rising UNRATE and FEDFUNDS above 4%, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative less than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in industrial production above 0.5% signal robust manufacturing activity and near-term economic acceleration, which quickly boosts investor sentiment and positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The fraction of positive 5-day SPX returns after INDPRO monthly increases above 0.5% is less than 55% over any 5-year rolling window.
